calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-03-2020 Off Calendar

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2020 Continued To Jun-16-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-21-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Contest The Good Faith Of Defendant Hamlin Hotel, L.P.S Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, the tentative ruling on DEFENDANT CARITAS MANAGEMENT CORPORATION's Motion To Contest The Good Faith Of Defendant Hamlin Hotel, L.P.'S Application For Good Faith Settlement Determination is adopted as follows:

Caritas Management Corporation's Motion to Contest Good Faith Settlement is granted. This ruling is without prejudice to settlors renewing their application for a good faith determination upon a proper evidentiary showing.

Although settlors assert this is a "miniscule" liability case in light of Caritas' role in hiring and supervising Mr. Bursey, they have not presented any admissible evidence that would enable the Court to determine "a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability." (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v., Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) "If 'there is no substantial evidence to support a critical assumption as to the nature and extent of a settling defendant's liability, then a determination of good faith based upon such assumption is an abuse of discretion.'" (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348 [settling parties did not proffer any evidence regarding their proportionate liability for plaintiff's alleged injury, but merely presented a series of "questionable assumptions" in their memorandum to show the settlement amount was reasonable].) Even if counsel had personal knowledge of such information, counsel's declaration does not provide any such evidence. (See Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834 [attorney's declaration regarding settling defendant's liability insufficient where he failed to provide specific supporting facts or expert opinion].) While the party asserting the lack of good faith has the burden on that issue (Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d)), the settlors must provide the court in the first instance with a sufficient evidentiary basis upon which to make a good faith determination. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(b) ["The issue of the good faith of a settlement may be determined on the basis of affidavits served with the notice of hearing, and any counteraffidavits filed in response . . . ."]; Mattco Forge, 38 Cal.App.4th at 1350, n.6.) Settlors have not done so.

Counsel for Caritas shall prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
